old_claim.note: Advanced Stone Tools is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: Glossed flint cereal-harvesting tools predate fully domesticated crops and supply a source-backed lithic harvesting predecessor without making stone tools the sole path to plant domestication.

source_shape.source_locator: The Ohalo II use-wear study reports glossed flint blades used for harvesting near-ripe wild cereals about 23,000 years ago.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports a lithic harvesting-tool predecessor for later crop cultivation and domestication.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The edge remains a historical predecessor because harvesting tools are part of the plant-use lineage but are not a universal hard requirement for every domesticated plant.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: advanced_stone_tools remains a historical predecessor edge with source support.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to vegetatively propagated tree crops only.
A specific cereal_harvesting_sickles node is added and should replace advanced_stone_tools.
A stronger source shows Ohalo II harvesting tools did not contribute to later cultivation lineages.
short_reason: Harvesting tools are a sourced predecessor, not a hard dependency.
